chapter_context.summary: A long and highly graphic allegory of Jerusalem as an abandoned infant girl whom the Lord rescued, raised, and married, only for her to play the harlot with neighboring nations, exceeding the wickedness of her sisters Samaria and Sodom, yet ending with the promise of an everlasting covenant.

outline.text: The Allegory of the Orphan Girl Saved, Adorned, and Wedded by God
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Allegory of the Orphan Girl Saved, Adorned, and Wedded by God (16:1-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Jerusalem's Shameless Harlotry with Foreign Nations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jerusalem's Shameless Harlotry with Foreign Nations (16:15-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Impending Judgment: Stripping and Slaughter by Her Lovers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Impending Judgment: Stripping and Slaughter by Her Lovers (16:35-43), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Direct Comparison of Sin with Sisters Sodom and Samaria
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Direct Comparison of Sin with Sisters Sodom and Samaria (16:44-59), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Promise of Future Restoration and Everlasting Covenant
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Promise of Future Restoration and Everlasting Covenant (16:60-63), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 16, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
